tool.name: QuPath
tool.description: interactive Open Software for Bioimage Analysis
tool.command: export HOME=\$PWD && ## Match the container user id to the host user/group id if supplied if [ -n "\$USERID" ]; then APP_USER=\$(getent passwd 1000 | cut -d: -f1) && usermod -u "\$USERID" "\$APP_USER"; fi && if [ -n "\$GROUPID" ]; then APP_GROUP=\$(getent group 1000 | cut -d: -f1) && groupmod -g "\$GROUPID" "\$APP_GROUP"; fi && mkdir -p ./outputs && chown "\${USERID:-1000}:\${GROUPID:-1000}" ./outputs || true && ## the app user of the container (in NFS export with root-squash this will fail and is not needed) ## the /opt/qupath/infile.tiff file is loaded automatically into the application ln -s '$infile' '/opt/qupath/infile.tiff' && /init
tool.help:
QuPath is a cross-platform software application designed for bioimage analysis - and specifically to meet the needs of whole slide image analysis and digital pathology.
QuPath aims to be user-friendly without compromising on power and flexibility so that it can handle tasks at all levels of complexity: from manually drawing regions and counting structures, to automatically detecting and interactively classifying hundreds of thousands of cells in huge images many gigabytes in size.
